summary: Smoking Help>Slip>Franklin and tight second‑set jams
blurb: The show shines with a blistering Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin’s Tower sequence and a powerful Wheel transition in the second set. Jerry delivers flawless guitar tone while Phil’s bass anchors dynamic jams. Highlights include Wharf Rat, Dancin’ In The Street and a tight drum duo.
review: July 12, 1976 at San Francisco’s intimate Orpheum Theatre captured one of the band’s classic ’76 outings. From the opening “The Music Never Stopped” through a bright “Lazy Lightning → Supplication” segue, the first set kept things rolling, but it was the second‑set that truly lifted the night: a smoking Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin’s Tower trio erupted with soaring bends and perfect guitar tone, leading into an energetic “Dancin’ In The Street,” a heartfelt “Wharf Rat,” and a thunderous drum duo that launched a seamless transition into “The Wheel” and a rollicking “Around & Around.”  Keith’s piano added depth throughout, especially on the Wheel transition, while Phil’s bass shines in the matrix mix, giving the whole performance a warm, forward thrust. Donna’s vocal presence was occasionally noted as intrusive, but overall the band feels relaxed yet euphoric, delivering one of the stronger shows of the year.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings are clearly positive, praising the Help/Slip/Franklin sequence, strong second‑set jams and tight drumming; three others are mixed, noting slower tempos, occasional vocal mic issues, and listless moments. The overall pattern leans positive with consistent high points across multiple sources, warranting a moderate +0.9 sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with solid, clear audio; captures the classic ’76 performance cleanly and highlights the second‑set jams best.

key_highlights: Smoking Help On The Way → Slipknot → Franklin’s Tower sequence with soaring guitar.
Powerful Wheel transition driven by an extended Bill/Mickey drum duo.
Tight, energetic renditions of Wharf Rat and Dancin’ In The Street.
Keith’s piano depth shines on the Wheel segue; Phil’s bass forward in matrix mix.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia delivers flawless guitar tone, especially in the second set; his bends on Franklin’s Tower are highlighted, though a few note Donna’s mic can drown out his vocals at times.
band_performance.Phil: Lesh’s bass subtly steers the band’s dynamics and shines forward in the matrix mix, providing a solid foundation throughout.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s backing vocals and occasional lead parts add texture, but some listeners feel his harmonies are over‑powered by Donna’s microphone.
band_performance.Keith: Keith Godchaux supplies solid piano work, adding depth on the Wheel transition and maintaining a steady groove through the jams.
band_performance.Mickey: Mickey Hart, alongside Bill Kreutzmann, delivers an extended, pure drum solo that propels the band into The Wheel with tight cohesion.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming locks in with Mickey, providing rhythmic drive especially during the high‑octane second set.
